reasoning: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates steady at 4.25%-4.5% at its May 7, 2025 meeting. According to the CME FedWatch tool, futures markets largely expect either a rate hold (around 60% probability) or a rate cut (around 40% probability) at the next FOMC meeting scheduled for June 17-18, 2025. A rate increase is essentially priced out of the market entirely, except as a microscopic tail risk. The Polymarket price on May 1st was 0.02, which perfectly reflects this remote tail probability of an unexpected hike due to a sudden and extreme inflation shock over the next month. Since the May meeting outcome reinforced the hold/cut narrative by citing rising uncertainties including unemployment, the probability of a hike has not grown and remains solidly at ~2%.
